content:
# How Long Is an MOT Valid? Your UK Guide to Car MOT Expiry Dates

For most cars, vans, and motorcycles in the UK, an MOT certificate is valid for **12 months** from the date it was issued, once your vehicle is over three years old. Understanding **how long an MOT is valid** is crucial for staying legal and safe on British roads. As car experts at BuyAndScrap.com, we’re here to give you straightforward advice on expiry dates, early tests, and more.

## The Standard MOT Validity Period: 12 Months

After its first three years, your car needs an annual MOT test. A pass means your certificate is valid for 12 months. This ensures your vehicle meets minimum road safety and environmental standards. Always check your current certificate for the exact expiry date.

### New Cars: The Three-Year Rule

Brand new cars are exempt from their first MOT for three years. The countdown starts from the vehicle's registration date. For example, a car registered on 1st March 2021 won't need its first MOT until 1st March 2024. After this initial test, it then falls into the standard 12-month cycle.

## Extending Your MOT: Testing Early

You can get your MOT done up to **one month (minus one day)** before your current certificate expires. If your vehicle passes, the new 12-month validity period will be added to your original expiry date. This effectively extends your MOT without losing any time.

**Example:** If your MOT expires on 20th May, you can test it from 21st April onwards. If it passes on 1st May, your new MOT will still expire on 20th May the following year. This is a smart way to keep your MOT renewal date consistent.

### Resetting Your MOT Expiry Date

If you get your MOT done *more than a month (minus one day)* before it expires, your new MOT certificate will be valid for 12 months from the date of the test. This resets your expiry date earlier. Consider this if you need an early test for a significant trip or sale, but be aware it shortens your current validity period.

## Driving Without a Valid MOT: The Risks

It is illegal to drive a vehicle on public roads without a valid MOT certificate. The only exception is if you're driving it directly to a pre-booked MOT test. Fines can reach up to £1,000, and your car insurance may become invalid in the event of an accident. Always ensure your MOT is up to date.

*Don't know your expiry date? Use our free [MOT Checker](/mot-checker) tool.*

## Common Questions About MOT Validity

Here are quick answers to frequently asked questions about **how long an MOT is valid**:

**Q: Can I drive my car on the road if my MOT has expired?**
A: No, it is illegal. You can only drive your car to a pre-booked MOT test at a garage, or to a place where it will be repaired.

**Q: How do I find out when my MOT expires?**
A: Your expiry date is printed on your current MOT certificate. You can also check it online using your vehicle's registration number via the UK government's free MOT history checker, or use our handy [Free MOT Checker](/mot-checker).

**Q: What happens if my car fails its MOT?**
A: If your car fails, you cannot drive it until the necessary repairs are made and it passes a retest. Driving a car with a major or dangerous fault found during an MOT is illegal and unsafe.

**Q: Is the MOT certificate digital or paper?**
A: While a paper certificate is usually issued, the official record is digital. You don't legally need to carry the paper certificate with you.

## Find Your Next Reliable Car

Understanding your car's MOT is key to responsible vehicle ownership. Whether you're buying your next car or selling your current one, having a valid MOT gives you peace of mind.
